objectactions.label: Object actions
objectactions.note: Verbs describing what you do to or with a physical object. Hungarian prefixes are strongly directional: fel- = upward, le- = downward, ki- = outward, be- = inward, össze- = together, szét- = apart, vissza- = back.

members.en: to hold; to catch; to grab

ex: She was arrested at the border.

members.en: to comprehend; to catch (a ball coming down)
ex: Did you grasp what I said?

families.sub: sustained holding or keeping in a state

members.en: to hold; to keep; to last; to contain
to keep; to retain; to hold (an event)
to persist, to persevere; to hold out
to hold together; to stick together

families.sub: pulling with force in a direction

members.en: to pull; to draw; to drag
to pull out, to extract; to cross out
ex: The dentist pulls out his tooth.
She crosses his name off the list.

members.en: to pull in; to draw inside
to pull up; to wind up; to build
to pull down; to strip off
to pull away; to drag aside
to cross out; to drag across
ex: He draws a line through it.

families.sub: applying force to move something forward

members.en: to push; to shove; to roll (wheels)
to push aside, to put aside
ex: They push the obstacle out of the way.

members.en: to push in, to slide in
ex: He pushes the car into the garage.

members.en: to push out; to extend (a deadline)

ex: He tosses one in the air.
He threw out his old clothes.

members.en: to throw in, to toss inside
ex: He throws it in the bin.
She throws the ball into the goal.
She was thrown out of her job.

members.en: to throw up; to toss up; to cheer up
to lift; to raise; to increase
to lift up; to pick up
to highlight; to lift out; to emphasise
to lift slightly; to raise (price, weight)

families.base: nyom — to press / to push down

members.en: to press; to push; to weigh
to press down; to push down
ex: He presses down the door handle.

members.en: to press in; to shove in
to squeeze out; to press out
to hit; to strike; to beat
to knock down (with vehicle); to hit away
ex: The car knocks down the pedestrian.

members.en: to knock down; to strike down; to type a key
to touch; to affect; to concern
to touch; to reach for; to tamper with
to place; to put; to stack
ex: She puts the books on the shelf.
He puts something in the bag.

members.en: to put down; to lay down
to put up; to load onto
ex: She puts it up on the shelf.

members.en: to put in; to place inside
ex: She puts it in the washing machine.
He puts it in the car.

members.en: to put out; to lay out; to display
to move from one place to another
ex: She moves it to the other shelf.

members.en: to tie; to knit; to bind
to tie up; to set (cement); to conclude (a deal)
to tie down; to secure; to engage (attention)
to moor; to stipulate a condition
ex: He moors the boat in the harbour.

families.sub: opening in different directions or ways

ex: The shop opens in the morning.

members.en: to open wide; to spread open
ex: She flings the window wide open.

members.en: to open up; to cut open
to close down; to shut in
to close off; to seal; to conclude

ex: They exclude him from the team.

members.en: to shut off (gas/water); to lock away
ex: She locks away the important documents.

families.sub: giving and handing in different directions

members.en: to hand over; to pass on
to give over to; to hand to someone
to give what is owed; to grant; to surrender
to give back; to return something

ex: She adds a spoon of salt.

members.en: to bring about; to deliver (a verdict)
to bring in; to import; to make up (a deficit)
to bring out; to get out
ex: She brings out the best in him.

members.en: to bring together; to arrange (a meeting)
to shake up; to shake (someone) by the shoulders
ex: She shakes her friend by the shoulders.

members.en: to shake out; to shake off

ex: She gives the bottle a good shake.

members.en: to twist; to screw; to wring
to screw in; to wrap up
ex: She screws in the light bulb.

members.en: to coil up; to twist together

movement.label: Movement & direction
movement.note: Direction is baked into the prefix. fel- = up, le- = down, ki- = out, be- = in, vissza- = back, el- = away, át- = across.

ex: She goes out through the door.
He goes out into the garden.
She goes down to the cellar.
He goes down into the metro.
He goes back to his room.
He gets out of the taxi.
I'm getting off at the next stop.

members.en: to take away, to carry off
ex: The wind blew away her hat.
It has to be taken back.

members.en: to take in, to bring inside
ex: She takes the child to school.
She turns into the car park.

members.en: to turn around; to occur to

ex: This has happened to me too.
It sometimes happens that she's late.
He turns back on the road.

speaking.label: Speaking & communication
speaking.note: meg- often adds completeness or formality to a speech act. el- means to relay or tell through. fel- can mean to raise a topic or report upward.

ex: He writes a lot in his diary.

members.en: to rewrite; to transfer in writing
ex: He transfers it to another name.

members.en: to compile, to make a list of
ex: They compile a list of participants.
I'll ask him when he's free.

families.sub: show to vs show off vs point at

ex: I'll show you where it is.

thinking.label: Thinking & deciding
thinking.note: meg- signals the completion of a mental act — understanding achieved, a decision made. el- often implies drifting into thought or becoming distracted.

families.sub: finding vs inventing vs figuring out

ex: He finds someone at the door.

members.en: to figure out; to make up
to plan out thoroughly in advance

physical.label: Physical actions
physical.note: Prefixes are very literal: ki- = outward, be- = inward, szét- = into pieces, össze- = together, le- = downward/off, fel- = upward.

members.en: to fill; to pour; to load
ex: He pours a glass of water.

members.en: to fill a role; to load into
to upload; to fill up; to charge
to reload; to recharge; to refresh

ex: She cuts the paper into pieces.
He cuts the cake into slices.

members.en: to mince, to chop up finely

statechange.label: State changes
statechange.note: meg- marks a completed change of state. el- can signal a negative drift or departure. ki- often implies coming out of a previous state.

members.en: to stand out; to break away
ex: She stands out from the group.
He stands out from the crowd.

members.en: to be cancelled; to fall behind
to remain, to persist, to survive
to fall behind; to miss out
ex: He falls behind in the race.

members.en: to use up, to utilise, to consume
ex: She uses up all the material.

members.en: to exploit, to take advantage of
ex: She takes advantage of her friend.

admin.label: Administrative & formal
admin.note: be- signals reporting inward to an authority. ki- means issuing outward. fel- often means reporting upward.

members.en: to announce; to report to authorities
to assign to a role or schedule
to partition, to divide up into parts
to commute in; to enter regularly
ex: He walks through the whole city.

emotions.label: Emotions & social
emotions.note: meg- often signals a completed change of emotional state. el- can suggest being drawn away by a feeling.

families.sub: calling by name vs phone vs challenging

members.en: to call by name; to call out

ex: They invited me to their party.
He waits at the bus stop.
A lot is expected of her.

families.sub: letting in vs letting go vs permitting

members.en: to let go; to release; to waive
ex: She lets go of his hand.

digital.label: Digital & tech
digital.note: Spatial metaphors applied digitally: fel- = upload, le- = download, be- = log in, ki- = log out.

members.en: to turn on; to switch on
ex: He switches on and starts working.

members.en: to log in; to check in
ex: She checks in at the hotel.

members.en: to log out; to check out
ex: He logs out of his account.
She checks out of the hotel.
